meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve can't deploy apps (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome can't deploy apps. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Git with AI: Solve Deployment Issues for Student Developers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment. Master Git with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code. A step-by-step guide for students to launch professional-grade apps.
content.answer_block: Deployment failure often stems from environment mismatches and uncommitted configuration drift. By integrating Git-based workflows with AI-driven debugging, you can automate infrastructure synchronization. Use Claude Code to analyze your repository's state against production requirements, then leverage Antigravity to generate valid deployment scripts that bridge the gap between local code and cloud environments.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect logic, but the moment you hit 'deploy,' silence. The terminal spits out cryptic errors, your environment variables are misaligned, and the production server remains an unreachable void. This is the 'deployment wall'—a rite of passage that separates casual coders from professional engineers.

But the era of manual debugging is over. By combining the semantic reasoning of Claude Code with the architectural precision of Antigravity, you can transform your deployment process from a source of anxiety into a repeatable, automated pipeline. Let’s turn your local project into a live, scalable reality.

steps.title: Repository Sanitization
steps.description: Before deploying, ensure your Git history is clean and your dependencies are strictly defined to prevent 'works on my machine' syndrome.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current repository structure and identify any missing configuration files or hardcoded environment variables that would prevent a clean deployment on a standard Linux production server.
steps.claude_code_command: claude review --path ./ --rules 'check for hardcoded secrets and missing .env.example files'

steps.title: Automated Environment Mapping
steps.description: Map your local environment variables to the production environment using AI to ensure compatibility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready Dockerfile and a corresponding .env.production template based on my current project structure and requirements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate production Dockerfile and env template based on current files'

steps.title: CI/CD Pipeline Orchestration
steps.description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that automates testing and deployment triggers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a GitHub Actions pipeline that triggers on push to the main branch, runs unit tests, and deploys to a cloud provider if the build passes.

steps.title: Deployment Hook Verification
steps.description: Use Claude to verify that your deployment hooks are correctly configured to handle database migrations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my database migration strategy and suggest a robust way to run migrations during the deployment phase without causing downtime.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'how should I handle database migrations in my deployment script to avoid downtime?'

steps.title: Final Production Smoke Test
steps.description: Run a final audit to ensure the production environment mirrors the local development environment perfectly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a checklist for a post-deployment smoke test that verifies API connectivity, database read/write access, and static asset delivery.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a post-deployment verification script'

pro_tips: Always use a .dockerignore file to prevent local node_modules from bloating your production container images.
Treat your infrastructure as code; if it isn't in Git, it doesn't exist for the production server.
Use semantic commit messages to allow AI agents to better index your deployment history during troubleshooting.
Implement a 'staging' branch that mirrors production exactly to catch environment drift before it hits live users.
Set up automated health checks that ping your root endpoint; if it fails, trigger an immediate Slack/Email alert.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, focus on monitoring. Install an observability tool like Sentry or LogRocket to track runtime errors, then iterate on your features using the same AI-driven Git workflow you mastered today.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting deployment errors hold back your potential as an engineer. You have the tools, the strategy, and the workflow to ship professional software. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
